channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gematsu.com

item.title: Rose & Camellia Collection ‘Online Battle Mode’ update launches in June
item.content:encoded:
• Switch

Slap-fight against players across the globe.

Publisher PLAYISM and developer Nigoro have will release a free update for slap battle action game Rose & Camellia Collection in June that adds an online battle mode, the companies announced.

Rose & Camellia Collection is available now for Switch.

Watch a new trailer below.

“Online Battle Mode” Update Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/05/rose-camellia-collection-online-battle-mode-update-launches-in-june
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Idol Manager: Virtual Venture announced for PC
item.content:encoded:
• PC

Pop star management simulation sequel due out in 2027.

Publisher PLAYISM and developer Glitch Pitch have announced Idol Manager: Virtual Venture, a sequel to the pop star management simulation game Idol Manager. It will launch in Early Access for PC via Steam in 2027 with English, Japanese, and Simplified Chinese language options, with additional languages to be determined.

Here is an overview of the game, via PLAYISM:

About

Climb to the top of the entertainment industry, no matter what it takes…

The idol agency management simulation Idol Manager, critically acclaimed for its exploration of the harsh realities of the pop idol industry, will finally become a series with this new entry, Idol Manager: Virtual Venture.

This time, your stars are virtual streamers! As a manager at a newly established streaming corporation, players will strive to take their streamers to the top of the virtual streaming industry.

With the same difficult management simulation as the last game, Virtual Venture introduces many new features specific to virtual streaming.

In the previous game, your agency only accepted female idols, but in Virtual Venture, male streamers are more than welcome! Help them customize their avatars, streaming assets, background music, and more to produce new and original virtual streamers for your agency.

Just like in Idol Manager, you can chat with your streamers to build up trust, but friendships aren’t the only type of relationship in your agency. Look out for internal conflicts within your streamer groups and watch as both the pretty and ugly sides of them emerge.

Will you lead your streamers to a bright, promising future, or a dark, grueling one? It’s up to you to bridge the gap between the virtual and the real and survive in this demanding industry. Will you be the producer of the world’s best virtual streamer?

Key Features

  • A fusion between management sim and visual novel with plenty of dark humor.
  • Hold auditions to scout streamers, from complete newbies to famous experienced indies (both male and female).
  • Create avatars, hand-pick stream assets, moderate chats, and select suitable music for your streamers for the best setup.
  • React to viewers in the chat with an interactive streaming system.
  • Expand your business and customize your agency to your liking.
  • Streamer dorm rooms that change depending on their characteristics.
  • Deepen your relationships with your streamers and observe relationships between the streamers as well, such as friendships, rivalries, romances, and internal conflicts.
  • Random events that affect your streamers’ careers, including spicy scandals.
  • Manage your streamers’ social media, decide what posts they like or reply to, and report bad actors trying to ruin your fun.
  • Chat with your streamers to get their opinion on you and gossip about relationships within the agency.
  • Both a full-fledged story mode and a management-focused freeplay mode.

Watch the announcement trailer below.

Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/05/idol-manager-virtual-venture-announced-for-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Deckbuilding autobattler Samurai Bringer: Rampage announced for PC
item.content:encoded:
Publisher PLAYISM and developer Alphawing have announced Samurai Bringer: Rampage, a deckbuilding autobattler entry in the Bringer series. It will launch for PC via Steam in 2026 with English, Japanese, Traditional Chinese, and Simplified Chinese language support.

Here is an overview of the game, via PLAYISM:

About

Alphawing, the developers of Samurai Bringer and Metal Bringer, will be releasing a new entry in the Bringer series—the deckbuilding autobattler Samurai Bringer: Rampage!

Samurai Bringer, the first game in the series, is a roguelite action game where you slay samurai and yokai alike in stages that change every run. It has gained over 900 reviews on Steam with a Very Positive rating.

Samurai Bringer: Rampage preserves the comical voxel samurai action of Samurai Bringer, but mixes things up with a brand new game system as a deckbuilding autobattler.

Players will select their samurai generals and build formations based on their samurai’s weapons and abilities. Fight your way through the war-ridden Sengoku period and come out on top to unite the land.

Form Your Army and Unite the Land

Arrange your samurai and strengthen your army to prepare for battle! Match weapon types and abilities to trigger synergies that enhance your formation. Adopt different strategies to add traits to your formation, focus on rushing the enemy early, or build up your strength over time. Survey the enemy’s formation and strategy, then rearrange your own forces to conquer the land!

Flashy Autobattling Fights

Battles are fought automatically against formations created by players from around the world and saved on the server. Enjoy chaotic battles with over-the-top, flashy attacks!

Choose Your General

Each General offers different starting samurai and strategies. Pick your favorite historical general, choose one whose strategy fits the formation you want to build, or even start from your preferred region. You are in command!

Game Modes

  • Hado (Supremacy) – Fight against formations made by players worldwide, raise your military merits, and surpass the gods of war.
  • Gido (Simulation) – Relaxing mock battles. Find the key to victory that works for you.

Watch the announcement trailer below.

Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/05/deckbuilding-autobattler-samurai-bringer-rampage-announced-for-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
